name: Airbnb
painPoints: Expensive hotel stays, lack of authentic local travel experiences, underutilized extra space in residential homes.
problem: Short-term lodging is overpriced and impersonal, while homeowners have no secure way to monetize their vacant space.
solution: A peer-to-peer marketplace that allows individuals to list their homes for travelers seeking unique, affordable accommodations.
market: Global travelers (budget and luxury) and homeowners with available space in urban and tourist destinations.
revenue: Service fees charged to both hosts (typically 3%) and guests (around 14%) per successful booking.

name: Stripe
painPoints: Complex payment gateway setups, high technical barriers for online payments, legacy banking systems slowing down internet commerce.
problem: Integrating online payments used to take weeks of legal work and complex coding, hindering the growth of the internet economy.
solution: Developer-first payment infrastructure and APIs that allow any business to accept payments instantly with just a few lines of code.
market: E-commerce startups, SaaS companies, and global internet platforms like Amazon and Shopify.
revenue: Transaction-based fee (typically 2.9% + 30¢) on every successful payment processed.

name: Slack
painPoints: Information silos, excessive internal emails, difficulty tracking team communications and file sharing.
problem: Modern team communication is fragmented across emails and multiple apps, leading to lost context and decreased productivity.
solution: A centralized, channel-based communication platform that integrates with thousands of third-party business tools.
market: Remote teams, tech startups, and large enterprises requiring real-time collaboration and transparency.
revenue: Freemium model with per-user monthly/yearly subscription tiers for message history and advanced features.

name: Duolingo
painPoints: Expensive language tutors, boring textbooks, lack of motivation for consistent daily practice.
problem: Learning a new language is often costly and time-consuming, with high dropout rates due to lack of engagement.
solution: A gamified language learning app that uses bite-sized lessons, streaks, and rewards to make learning addictive and free.
market: Global students, hobbyists, and professionals seeking to learn a new language on mobile devices.
revenue: Subscription-based 'Super Duolingo' (ad-free), display advertising for free users, and English proficiency test fees.

name: Peloton
painPoints: Inconvenience of commuting to gyms, lack of motivation in solo home workouts, expensive boutique fitness classes.
problem: People struggle to find time for the gym but find home workouts uninspiring without the energy of a live group class.
solution: High-end connected fitness hardware (bikes/treads) paired with a live-streamed and on-demand boutique class subscription.
market: Affluent fitness enthusiasts and busy professionals seeking a premium gym experience at home.
revenue: High-margin hardware sales (Bikes/Treads) plus a recurring monthly digital membership fee.
